title: Mahtari Vandana Yojana (Chhattisgarh) — ₹1,000/Month for Women
meta_description: Mahtari Vandana Yojana CG 2026 — ₹1,000/month for 70+ lakh married women in Chhattisgarh. Eligibility, documents, apply online, payment status.

hero.one_liner: ₹1,000 monthly direct cash transfer to married, widowed, divorced, and separated women aged 23-60 in Chhattisgarh — 70+ lakh women already enrolled

what_is_it: Mahtari Vandana Yojana is a direct cash transfer scheme launched on 1 March 2024 by the Chhattisgarh government under CM Vishnu Deo Sai. The state transfers ₹1,000 every month (₹12,000/year) directly into the bank accounts of eligible women. Over 70 lakh women across the state are already enrolled and receiving payments via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

The scheme covers married, widowed, divorced, and separated women aged 23-60 who are permanent residents of Chhattisgarh with family income below ₹2.5 lakh/year. Your name must appear in Panchayat records or the BPL list. The money goes straight to your Aadhaar-linked bank account — no middleman, no office visits to collect it.

As of December 2024, the government has successfully transferred 10 monthly installments. Payments typically land between the 15th and 25th of each month. This is a recurring benefit — not a one-time payment. As long as you remain eligible, you continue receiving ₹1,000 every month.

Mahtari Vandana is part of a nationwide trend of state-level women cash transfer schemes. Madhya Pradesh's Ladli Behna gives ₹1,250/month, Maharashtra's Majhi Ladki Bahin gives ₹1,500/month, and Karnataka's Gruha Lakshmi gives ₹2,000/month. These are different state schemes — you can only apply in the state where you're a permanent resident.

eligibility.label: Family income
eligibility.value: Annual income must not exceed ₹2.5 lakh

eligibility.label: Panchayat records
eligibility.value: Name must appear in Panchayat records or BPL list

eligibility.label: Bank account
eligibility.value: Must have bank account linked with Aadhaar for DBT

eligibility.label: Government job exclusion
eligibility.value: No family member (spouse, children, parents) should hold a government job

eligibility.label: No duplicate benefits
eligibility.value: Cannot receive similar cash transfer from other central/state scheme

extra_sections.heading: Comparison With Similar Schemes in Other States
Who Cannot Apply — Exclusions
content: You are NOT eligible for Mahtari Vandana if any of these apply:

Government employment

Any immediate family member (spouse, children, parents) holds a government job — central, state, or local body. This includes contractual government positions in some cases.

Duplicate scheme benefits

You're already receiving a similar cash transfer benefit from any other central or state scheme. You must choose one — you cannot claim both.

Income above limit

Your family's total annual income from all sources exceeds ₹2.5 lakh. Income is verified through the income certificate issued by Gram Panchayat or Tehsildar.

Not in Panchayat records

Your name does not appear in either Panchayat village records or the official BPL list. If missing, contact your Gram Panchayat to get included first.

Non-resident

You are not a permanent resident of Chhattisgarh. Temporary residents or migrants from other states are not eligible.

Age outside range

You are below 23 years or above 60 years of age as per your Aadhaar card.

extra_sections.heading: Documents Required
rows: Must be linked with your bank account
Issued by Gram Panchayat or Tehsildar. Valid for 1 year
First page with account number. Account must be in your name
BPL card preferred, APL also accepted
From Gram Panchayat or municipal corporation
Court-issued divorce decree or spouse's death certificate
Recent color photo, not older than 6 months

extra_sections.heading: Payment Schedule and How to Check Status
content: Payments are credited between the 15th and 25th of each month via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) to your Aadhaar-linked bank account. As of December 2024, 10 installments have been successfully transferred to 70+ lakh women across Chhattisgarh.

How to check payment status

Visit mahtarivandan.cgstate.gov.in and log in with your Aadhaar number to view all successful transfers, pending payments, and any rejection reasons. You can also check your bank passbook or mobile banking app for deposits.

Payment delayed?

Common reasons: Aadhaar-bank account mismatch, incomplete KYC, or income certificate expired. Contact your nearest Anganwadi center or the District Women and Child Development office.
Ensure your Aadhaar is properly linked to your bank account — this is the #1 reason for payment failures.

how_to_apply.title: Check your eligibility
how_to_apply.description: Verify: age 23-60, married/widowed/divorced, CG permanent resident, family income ≤₹2.5L, no government job in family, name in Panchayat/BPL records.

how_to_apply.title: Gather documents
how_to_apply.description: Aadhaar card, income certificate, bank passbook, ration card, marriage/divorce/death certificate (as applicable), passport photo. Ensure Aadhaar is linked with your bank account.

how_to_apply.title: Apply online or at Anganwadi center
how_to_apply.description: Online: Visit mahtarivandan.cgstate.gov.in → click 'Apply' → fill form → upload documents → submit. Offline: Visit your nearest Anganwadi center with all documents — the worker will fill and submit the form for you.

how_to_apply.title: Track application
how_to_apply.description: You'll receive a reference number via SMS. Use it to track status on the portal. Processing takes 15-30 days. If documents need verification, Gram Panchayat or Anganwadi worker will contact you.

how_to_apply.title: Receive monthly payments
how_to_apply.description: Once approved, ₹1,000 is credited to your bank account every month starting the next payment cycle (15th-25th). Check your bank balance or the portal to confirm receipt.

important_alert.text: The only official website is mahtarivandan.cgstate.gov.in. Beware of fake websites and agents claiming to help you get faster approvals. The government will NEVER ask for money upfront. Do not share your Aadhaar, bank details, or OTP with anyone except through the official portal or at your Anganwadi center.

faqs.question: Is this the same as Ladli Behna?
faqs.answer: Same concept, different state. Mahtari Vandana is Chhattisgarh (₹1,000/mo). Ladli Behna is Madhya Pradesh (₹1,250/mo). Majhi Ladki Bahin is Maharashtra (₹1,500/mo). You can only apply in the state where you're a permanent resident.

faqs.question: My husband has a government job. Can I apply?
faqs.answer: No. The scheme disqualifies women whose family members (spouse, children, parents) hold government jobs at any level — central, state, or local body.

faqs.question: I'm a widow. What extra documents do I need?
faqs.answer: You need your deceased spouse's death certificate issued by the municipal corporation or Gram Panchayat, in addition to all standard documents (Aadhaar, income certificate, bank passbook, ration card, photo).

faqs.question: What if my name is not in Panchayat records?
faqs.answer: Contact your Gram Panchayat office to get your name included. This typically takes a few weeks. You can also qualify if your name appears in the BPL list instead. Apply only after your name appears in one of these records.

faqs.question: Can I get this along with other government benefits?
faqs.answer: You cannot receive Mahtari Vandana if you're already getting a similar direct cash transfer from another scheme. However, non-cash benefits (ration card, Ayushman Bharat health insurance, LPG subsidy) do not disqualify you.

faqs.question: How do I check if my payment was credited?
faqs.answer: Three ways: (1) Log into mahtarivandan.cgstate.gov.in with your Aadhaar number. (2) Check your bank passbook or mobile banking app for deposits between 15th-25th. (3) Ask at your Anganwadi center with your reference number.

faqs.question: What if my payment is delayed?
faqs.answer: Usually caused by Aadhaar-bank account mismatch, expired income certificate, or incomplete KYC. Visit your Anganwadi center or district WCD office to identify and fix the issue. Ensure your Aadhaar is linked to the correct bank account.

faqs.question: Can I reapply if rejected?
faqs.answer: Yes. Fix the reason for rejection (usually incomplete documents or name not in records) and resubmit. There's no limit on reapplications. The portal tells you why you were rejected.
